title: lessingia imbricata
intro: Lessingia imbricata, also known as Coyote Brush, is a flowering plant native to the coastal regions of the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including sore throats, coughs, and respiratory problems, as well as to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The herbal preparations of Lessingia imbricata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its health benefits.
